title: Unify remote-aware base branch discovery
outOfScope: Changing PR provider or merge policy
Changing release version or publishing a tag

sources.reason: Review finding P2-2 identifies inconsistent base branch discovery between ai-finish and ai-close-work-item.
Current finish-stage discovery collapses remote candidates by branch name.
Current close-stage discovery is the canonical remote-aware behavior to share.

riskAssessment.reason: Finish and close must reject the same ambiguous remote default-branch state, including two remotes that advertise the same branch name.

executionDecision.reason: User confirmed the remediation loop; the shared helper and regression tests are well-defined.

preReviewWarnings: Verify ai-finish and ai-close-work-item use the same remote-aware candidate identity and fail closed on duplicate remote HEADs.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A shared remote-aware discovery helper defines candidates as (remote, default branch), and both finish and close call it.
ai-finish and ai-close-work-item reject multiple remote HEAD targets with the same branch name instead of silently choosing one.
Regression tests cover one remote, no remote HEAD, distinct remote defaults, and duplicate branch names across remotes.
The implementation preserves the existing fail-closed diagnostic and does not change release or adopter configuration behavior.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.
Lifecycle boundary discovery must use one shared remote-aware implementation.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: one remote advertises main and both lifecycle phases select it
no remote HEAD is configured and finish remains permissive
two remotes advertise different default branches and both phases fail closed
two remotes advertise the same branch name and both phases fail closed

intent.problem: ai-finish and ai-close-work-item currently implement subtly different remote default-branch discovery rules, so a multi-remote repository can pass one lifecycle phase and fail the next or select the wrong base.
constraints: Keep fail-closed behavior for ambiguous remote HEAD state.
Preserve existing public diagnostics and lifecycle order.
Do not hard-code origin/main or provider-specific behavior.
intent.rationale: A shared helper makes the lifecycle contract deterministic and ensures both pre-PR finish and post-merge close interpret remote identity the same way.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly confirmed the remediation loop, including lifecycle governance fixes.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
